metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Arts Education 8 is an integrated arts course encompassing dance, drama, music, and visual arts. Students explore creative expression as a means of communicating identity, culture, and perspective, developing artistic skills across multiple disciplines. First Peoples artistic traditions and diverse cultural works are central to the curriculum, and students engage thoughtfully with the ethics of artistic practice including cultural appropriation. This course builds the foundation for senior Arts pathways.
metadata.general_description: An integrated arts course exploring dance, drama, music, and visual arts as unique languages for creative expression, cultural connection, and personal identity — developing artistic skills, reflective practice, and appreciation for diverse artistic traditions.

big_ideas.statement: Creative growth requires patience, readiness to take risks, and willingness to try new approaches.
big_ideas.elaboration: Developing as an artist means embracing the discomfort of not knowing — being willing to experiment, fail, revise, and persist. Creative risk-taking is essential to artistic growth across all art forms.

big_ideas.statement: Dance, drama, music, and visual arts are each unique languages for creating and communicating.
big_ideas.elaboration: Each art form has its own vocabulary, conventions, and expressive possibilities — body and movement in dance, character and narrative in drama, sound and rhythm in music, and visual elements in art — all of which can express what words alone cannot.

big_ideas.statement: Individuals and communities express themselves through the arts.
big_ideas.elaboration: Artistic expression is both personal and communal — rooted in identity, place, and culture. The arts provide a means for individuals and groups to share their stories, values, and experiences with the world.

big_ideas.statement: Artists challenge established perspectives and expose us to new viewpoints.
big_ideas.elaboration: Art has always been a vehicle for social commentary, cultural critique, and the expansion of human understanding. Engaging with challenging artworks develops critical thinking and empathy.

competencies.raw: Intentionally select and apply materials, movements, technologies, and techniques by combining artistic elements and principles to express moods, ideas, and messages; create artistic works collaboratively and individually using imagination, inquiry, and experimentation; explore relationships between identity, place, culture, and belonging; demonstrate understanding of personal, social, cultural, and environmental contexts in the arts.
i_can_statements: I can intentionally choose and combine artistic elements, materials, and techniques to express specific ideas and moods.
I can create artworks collaboratively and individually through experimentation and imaginative inquiry.
I can explore how my identity, place, and cultural background influence and appear in my artistic work.
I can demonstrate understanding of the personal, social, cultural, and environmental contexts that shape artistic work.

competencies.raw: Describe and analyze how artists use processes, materials, and technologies to create and communicate; develop and critically appraise own and others' ideas to improve artistic quality; reflect on artworks to understand artists' motivations; interpret works using knowledge from various learning areas; respond to artworks using worldly knowledge and personal experience.
i_can_statements: I can describe and analyze how artists use materials and techniques to communicate meaning.
I can critically appraise my own and others' artistic work to identify strengths and areas for improvement.
I can reflect on artworks to interpret the artist's intentions and the context in which they were created.
I can respond to artworks by connecting them to knowledge from other subject areas and personal experiences.

competencies.raw: Adapt skills, processes, materials, movements, and techniques for new contexts and audiences; interpret and communicate ideas using symbols, elements, and principles; take creative risks in expressing feelings, ideas, and experiences; describe and respond to artworks; document, choreograph, perform, and share creative works; use arts to address environmental and global issues.
i_can_statements: I can adapt my artistic skills and techniques for different audiences and contexts.
I can communicate ideas using symbols, imagery, and artistic elements intentionally.
I can take creative risks in expressing my own feelings, perspectives, and experiences.
I can document, perform, and share my artistic work in thoughtful and purposeful ways.
I can use artistic expression to engage with environmental and global issues.

areas.name: Dance
areas.raw: Elements, principles, and skills of dance.
topics: Elements of dance: body, space, time, dynamics, and relationships
Basic technique skills: alignment, weight transfer, balance, and coordination
Exploration of movement styles: contemporary, hip hop, traditional/cultural dance forms
Choreographic tools: improvisation, call and response, unison, canon
First Peoples dance traditions: significance, protocol, and respectful engagement
Performer and audience roles in dance contexts

areas.name: Drama
areas.raw: Elements, conventions, and techniques of drama.
topics: Elements of drama: character, time, place, plot, tension, mood, and focus
Drama conventions: freeze frame, hot seating, thought tracking, role on the wall
Voice and body in performance: projection, articulation, movement, and gesture
Improvisation: spontaneous response, building scenes collaboratively
Devised and scripted theatre: reading, interpreting, and performing
First Peoples storytelling and performance traditions: oral history and theatrical form

areas.name: Music
areas.raw: Elements, notation, and performance skills in music.
topics: Elements of music: rhythm, melody, harmony, dynamics, tempo, texture, timbre, form
Notation literacy: reading and writing basic notation in treble and/or bass clef
Performance skills: technique, expression, and ensemble participation
Music history and cultural contexts: Indigenous music, world music traditions
Music and technology: introduction to basic recording and production tools
Songwriting and composition: creating short musical pieces or variations

areas.name: Visual Arts
areas.raw: Elements, principles, media, and processes of visual arts.
topics: Elements of art: line, shape, form, colour, value, texture, space
Principles of design: balance, contrast, emphasis, pattern, rhythm, unity, variety
Media and processes: drawing, painting, printmaking, collage, sculpture, mixed media
Introduction to photography and digital art
Art history: significant artworks and artists from diverse cultures and time periods
First Peoples visual art traditions: form lines, cultural symbolism, and Northwest Coast art

areas.name: Ethics and Contexts
areas.raw: Ethical considerations in the arts including cultural appropriation and copyright.
topics: Cultural appropriation vs. cultural appreciation: understanding the difference
Copyright and intellectual property in the arts
First Peoples protocols: asking permission and showing respect
Social responsibility in creating and sharing artwork
Representation and diversity in the arts
